quote_of_day.text:
>>17575
I was feeling miserable today, but I got up and did something, and I feel okay. I'm lonely, that's the thing. I guess its just a period of life, I live, a very strange life, in a sense I have been blessed entirely. I have things I cherish so much, and I feel, wonderful to be me, but.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of familial tension moved into the board, as Anon recounted a strained family engagement gathering. The system brought squalls of resentment and disagreements over values and relationships.

A scattered drizzle of genetic discussions fell across the board, with Anon exploring the genetic components of Indian castes.

The conversation was marked by a high-pressure ridge of data and statistics, as Anon shared findings on steppe genes among various castes and tribes.

A cold snap of complaints chilled the board, as Anon griped about late-night noise from neighbors. The chill was accompanied by a fog of frustration, with Anon venting about the disruptions.

Meanwhile, a brief but intense downpour of physical descriptions occurred, with Anon detailing a Punjabi poster's appearance. A drought of introspection was briefly alleviated as Anon mused on upper caste 'privilege'.

articles.title: Rich relatives' engagement gathering sparks tensions over values and relationships
articles.body:
Anon described attending a 10-day engagement event hosted by a wealthy relative, where they felt out of place among liberal and what they perceived as 'anti-national' relatives.

The relative, referred to as 'randwa sahab,' was said to have a YouTube channel and was flexing his philosophical knowledge, citing thinkers like Evola and Spinoza. Anon claimed to have tried discussing Indian philosophy but was laughed at.

Anon also mentioned a cousin dating a 'literal mulla' living in London. Anon expressed discomfort with the lavish lifestyle and what they saw as promiscuous behavior, including a female cousin bringing her boyfriend and allegedly engaging in public displays of affection.

They also described being forced to sleep on the floor while the relative and his girlfriend used a luxurious bathroom. Replies suggested Anon came from a middle-class background and a family with different values.

One respondent noted their family had historically been Brahmin Pandits but were now considered 'naam ke savarna' due to past actions. Anon received little sympathy, with one reply suggesting they were 'lowborn' and should focus on hard work rather than expecting special treatment.

The discussion turned to potential actions against the relative and his girlfriend, with some anons suggesting violence or other forms of retaliation.

Others questioned the familial connections and possible shared bloodlines with the 'mulla lover.' The thread included calls for help and suggestions to 'ruin' the relative's life. The overall tone was one of discomfort and clash between perceived values and lifestyles.

Anon's account painted a picture of a tense family gathering with strong feelings of disconnection and disapproval.

articles.title: Genetic components of Indian castes discussed
articles.body:
Punjabi posted a list of claims about the genetic components of various Indian castes and regions.

They stated that OBCs of Punjab, such as Saina and Tarkhan, have more steppe genes than upper castes of UP, Rajasthan, Himachal, and Uttrakhand.

Anon replied that steppe genes are not significant, referencing a user named Kanglu who allegedly discussed Turkish and Muslim genetics.

Another anon noted that UP, Bihari, and Rajasthani Brahmins, such as Gaur, Kanyakubja, Saryuparin, and Bhumihar, have high steppe percentages.

Himachali gunda provided information on the genetic trends of various regions, stating that Gangetic Brahmins have higher AASI and lower Zagros than Pakistanis and NW Indians. They also mentioned that Kashmiris have higher AASI than Punjabis.

According to him, Zagros is low in the Gangetic plains, but higher in South Indian landlord castes. Anon questioned the validity of these claims, asking about the source and sample size.

Another anon mentioned that Jaats are considered OBCs and have high steppe genes.

The discussion also touched on the AASI component, with Himachali gunda stating that it's a "bigger joke" as it clubs diverse genetically drifted components together due to lack of proxies.

articles.title: Anon Complains About Late-Night Noise from Neighbors
articles.body:
Anon posted about being disturbed by loud songs with harmonium and bhagwat slop coming from Hindu neighbors at 3 am. They contrasted this with Mullahs, who they claim only perform namaz in the evening.

Another anon suggested that the poster simply tell their neighbors that the noise is a nuisance, citing a personal experience where they and their neighbors agreed to limit construction noise to outside of nighttime hours.

A respondent told the original poster to consider moving to a different area if they want peace and quiet, suggesting that they live in a predominantly Hindu society.

One anon expressed frustration that they likely can't do anything about the noise since the neighbors probably have permission from local authorities.

Some anons engaged in a brief discussion about the nature of India as a Hindu nation, with one stating that it's 'the Handu's nation' and others responding with disagreement or sarcasm.

articles.title: Punjabi poster's physical description
articles.body: Punjabi described themselves as having light brown hair, a pale complexion with pink undertones, and a soft body with a floral smell. Anon asked about the Bharat Bhushan Tiwari case, inquiring if Bihar police targets specific castes and protects others. Another anon mentioned a user resembling a Bihari Baman. Punjabi responded to a query about their skin tone, stating they are light brown like many Jats and Khatris.

articles.title: Anon muses on upper caste 'privilege'
articles.body:
Anon started a thread asking what the average Indiachan member thinks about being upper caste. One respondent claimed their ancestors were 'smart and hardworking' and that is why they are privileged.

Another anon brought up historical practices such as dola pratha and mulakkaram, which they described as oppressive customs.

The discussion quickly devolved into personal attacks and insults, with one poster saying upper castes are a 'curse' and should be 'culled'.
